revision.text:
'''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[bridges]] crossing the [[Sumida River]], a [[class A river]] in [[Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the [[Meiji period]], the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): [[Ina Tadatsugu]], the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the [[Great Fire of Meireki]], the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the [[:en:Ryōgoku_Bridge|Ryōgoku Bridge]]. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.

'''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[bridges]] crossing the [[Sumida River]], a [[class A river]] in [[Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the [[Meiji period]], the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): [[Ina Tadatsugu]], the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the [[Great Fire of Meireki]], the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the [[:en:Ryōgoku_Bridge|Ryōgoku Bridge]]. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}

'''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[bridges]] crossing the [[Sumida River]], a [[class A river]] in [[Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the [[Meiji period]], the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): [[Ina Tadatsugu]], the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the [[Great Fire of Meireki]], the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the [[:en:Ryōgoku_Bridge|Ryōgoku Bridge]]. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.

=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}

revision.text:
'''隅田川橋梁群'''（すみだがわきょうりょうぐん）は、[[一級河川]]である[[隅田川]]に架かる[[橋梁]]群。[[江戸幕府]]により5橋が架橋され、明治期に入ると大幅に増えた。2020年現在、30以上の道路橋や鉄道橋が架かっており、このうち26橋が徒歩で渡れる<ref>[author=伊藤隆太郎 |https://withnews.jp/article/f0200109000qq000000000000000W05910101qq000020344A 隅田川の「歩ける橋」をぜんぶ渡る]朝日新聞社withnews</ref>。
==歴史== ===江戸時代=== *1594年（文禄3年）、関東郡代[[伊奈忠次]]は[[千住大橋]]を架橋する。 *1661年（寛文元年）、[[明暦の大火]]を受けて江戸幕府は[[両国橋]]を架橋する。 *元禄年間、江戸幕府は[[新大橋]]と[[永代橋]]を架橋する。 *1774年（安永3年）町方により[[吾妻橋]]が架橋される。 <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|千住大橋 File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|両国橋 File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|新大橋 File:100 views edo 004.jpg|永代橋 File:100 views edo 055.jpg|吾妻橋 </gallery>
===明治時代=== *1872年（明治5年）、[[永代橋]]、[[新大橋]]、[[両国橋]]、[[吾妻橋]]の4橋が明治政府の直轄橋梁に指定される。 *1874年（明治7年）、[[厩橋]]を架橋する。 *1903年（明治36年）、[[相生橋 (東京都)|相生橋]]を架橋する。
===大正時代=== [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|復興された清洲橋。隅田十橋のうちで最もモダンで美しいと人気があった]] *1913年（大正3年）、[[白鬚橋]]を架橋する。 *[[関東大震災]]で木造橋が焼失。多数の避難民が避難路を絶たれ焼死したことから鉄橋の架橋が計画される。 **[[内務省復興局]]、'''隅田川六橋'''（永代橋、[[清洲橋]]、[[蔵前橋]]、[[駒形橋]]、[[言問橋]]、[[相生橋 (東京都)|相生橋]]）を復興する。 **[[東京市]]、[[両国橋]]、[[厩橋]]、吾妻橋を復興する。
===昭和前期=== *[[東京大空襲]]で重要な避難路の一つとなる。
===戦後～平成期===
==現在の主な道路橋== ===戦前架橋=== ;重要文化財 {{col| *永代橋 *清洲橋 | *[[勝鬨橋]] }} ;東京都選定歴史的建造物 {{col| *蔵前橋 *厩橋 *駒形橋 *吾妻橋 | *[[白鬚橋]] *両国橋 *言問橋 }} ;その他 *[[千住大橋]](旧橋)
===戦後架橋=== {{col| *千住大橋(新橋) *[[水神大橋]] *[[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] *[[新大橋]] | *[[隅田川大橋]] *相生橋 *[[中央大橋]] *[[佃大橋]] }}
<!-- ===移築現存旧橋=== -->
== 脚注 == <references />
==参考文献== *[[陣内秀信]]「東京」[[文藝春秋]]
==関連項目== *[[隅田川橋梁]] *[[勝鬨橋]] *[[後藤新平]] *[[ビスカヤ橋]] *[[ソコルル・メフメト・パシャ橋]]
==外部リンク== * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/KISHINO-H/main/sumidar-mn/sumidar-hst.htm |title=隅田川の歴史 |date=20120403120058}} * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/uraisan/47/4702sumida.html |title=隅田川の橋梁群と両岸の下町地域 |date=20040621143724}}
{{隅田川の橋}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみたかわきようりようくん}} [[Category:東京都区部の橋]] [[Category:隅田川の橋|*]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸幕府]]

'''隅田川橋梁群'''（すみだがわきょうりょうぐん）は、[[一級河川]]である[[隅田川]]に架かる[[橋梁]]群。[[江戸幕府]]により5橋が架橋され、明治期に入ると大幅に増えた。2020年現在、30以上の道路橋や鉄道橋が架かっており、このうち26橋が徒歩で渡れる<ref>[author=伊藤隆太郎 |https://withnews.jp/article/f0200109000qq000000000000000W05910101qq000020344A 隅田川の「歩ける橋」をぜんぶ渡る]朝日新聞社withnews</ref>。
==歴史== ===江戸時代=== *1594年（文禄3年）、関東郡代[[伊奈忠次]]は[[千住大橋]]を架橋する。 *1661年（寛文元年）、[[明暦の大火]]を受けて江戸幕府は[[両国橋]]を架橋する。 *元禄年間、江戸幕府は[[新大橋]]と[[永代橋]]を架橋する。 *1774年（安永3年）町方により[[吾妻橋]]が架橋される。 <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|千住大橋 File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|両国橋 File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|新大橋 File:100 views edo 004.jpg|永代橋 File:100 views edo 055.jpg|吾妻橋 </gallery>
===明治時代=== *1872年（明治5年）、[[永代橋]]、[[新大橋]]、[[両国橋]]、[[吾妻橋]]の4橋が明治政府の直轄橋梁に指定される。 *1874年（明治7年）、[[厩橋]]を架橋する。 *1903年（明治36年）、[[相生橋 (東京都)|相生橋]]を架橋する。
===大正時代=== [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|復興された清洲橋。隅田十橋のうちで最もモダンで美しいと人気があった]] *1913年（大正3年）、[[白鬚橋]]を架橋する。 *[[関東大震災]]で木造橋が焼失。多数の避難民が避難路を絶たれ焼死したことから鉄橋の架橋が計画される。 **[[内務省復興局]]、'''隅田川六橋'''（永代橋、[[清洲橋]]、[[蔵前橋]]、[[駒形橋]]、[[言問橋]]、[[相生橋 (東京都)|相生橋]]）を復興する。 **[[東京市]]、[[両国橋]]、[[厩橋]]、吾妻橋を復興する。
===昭和前期=== *[[東京大空襲]]で重要な避難路の一つとなる。
===戦後～平成期===
==現在の主な道路橋== ===戦前架橋=== ;重要文化財 {{col| *永代橋 *清洲橋 | *[[勝鬨橋]] }} ;東京都選定歴史的建造物 {{col| *蔵前橋 *厩橋 *駒形橋 *吾妻橋 | *[[白鬚橋]] *両国橋 *言問橋 }} ;その他 *[[千住大橋]](旧橋)
===戦後架橋=== {{col| *千住大橋(新橋) *[[水神大橋]] *[[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] *[[新大橋]] | *[[隅田川大橋]] *相生橋 *[[中央大橋]] *[[佃大橋]] }}
<!-- ===移築現存旧橋=== -->
== 脚注 == <references />
==参考文献== *[[陣内秀信]]「東京」[[文藝春秋]]
==関連項目== *[[隅田川橋梁]] *[[勝鬨橋]] *[[後藤新平]] *[[ビスカヤ橋]] *[[ソコルル・メフメト・パシャ橋]]
==外部リンク== * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/KISHINO-H/main/sumidar-mn/sumidar-hst.htm |title=隅田川の歴史 |date=20120403120058}} * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/uraisan/47/4702sumida.html |title=隅田川の橋梁群と両岸の下町地域 |date=20040621143724}}
{{隅田川の橋}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみたかわきようりようくん}} [[Category:東京都区部の橋]] [[Category:隅田川の橋|*]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸幕府]]

'''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[bridges]] crossing the [[Sumida River]], a [[class A river]] in [[Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the [[Meiji period]], the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): [[Ina Tadatsugu]], the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the [[Great Fire of Meireki]], the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the [[:en:Ryōgoku_Bridge|Ryōgoku Bridge]]. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * [[Jinnai Hidenobu]] "Tokyo" [[Bungeishunju]].
== Related Articles == * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] * [[Gotō Shinpei]] * [[Vizcaya Bridge]] * [[Sokollu Mehmed Pasha Bridge]]
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/KISHINO-H/main/sumidar-mn/sumidar-hst.htm |title=History of the Sumida River |date=20120403120058}} * {{Wayback|url=http://homepage1.nifty.com/uraisan/47/4702sumida.html |title=Sumida River Bridge Group and the Downtown Districts Along Its Banks |date=20040621143724}}

<!--Sumida River Bridges--><!--Sumida River Bridges--><!--Sumida River Bridges--><!--Sumida River Bridges--> [[Category:Bridges in the special wards of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokugawa shogunate]] [[Category:Bridges over the Sumida River]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11658850}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:隅田川橋梁群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11658850]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[:en:bridges|bridges]] crossing the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], a [[:en:Class A river|Class A river]] in [[:en:Tokyo, Japan|Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): [[:en:Ina Tadatsugu|Ina Tadatsugu]], the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the [[:en:Great Fire of Meireki|Great Fire of Meireki]], the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the [[:en:Ryōgoku_Bridge|Ryōgoku Bridge]]. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[:en:Great Kantō Earthquake|Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * [[Jinnai Hidenobu]] "Tokyo" [[:en:Bungeishunju|Bungeishunju]].
== Related Articles == * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] * [[:en:Gotō Shinpei|Gotō Shinpei]] * [[:en:Vizcaya Bridge|Vizcaya Bridge]] * [[Sokollu Mehmed Pasha Bridge]]

=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.

=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[:en:Great Kantō Earthquake|Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}

Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11658850}} [[Category:Bridges in the special wards of Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges over the Sumida River]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Tokugawa shogunate]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:隅田川橋梁群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:隅田川橋梁群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:隅田川橋梁群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11658850]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[:en:bridges|bridges]] crossing the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], a [[:en:Class A river|Class A river]] in [[:en:Tokyo, Japan|Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): [[:en:Ina Tadatsugu|Ina Tadatsugu]], the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the [[:en:Great Fire of Meireki|Great Fire of Meireki]], the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the [[:en:Ryōgoku_Bridge|Ryōgoku Bridge]]. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[:en:Great Kantō Earthquake|Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}

=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the [[:en:Great Kantō Earthquake|Great Kantō Earthquake]], wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The [[:en:Tokyo_City|Tokyo City Government]] reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the [[:en:Bombing_of_Tokyo|Tokyo Air Raids]], the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}

Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The {{ill|Tokyo_City|en|Tokyo_City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City Government}} reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the {{ill|Bombing_of_Tokyo|en|Bombing_of_Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo Air Raids}}, the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.

'''Sumida River Bridge Group''' (隅田川橋梁群) refers to the collection of [[:en:bridges|bridges]] crossing the {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River}}, a [[:en:Class A river|Class A river]] in [[:en:Tokyo, Japan|Tokyo, Japan]]. Originally, five bridges were constructed by the [[Tokugawa shogunate|Tokugawa Shogunate]], and during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the number of bridges increased significantly. As of 2020, there are over 30 road and rail bridges spanning the Sumida River, 26 of which are walkable<ref>[author=Ryutaro Ito |https://withnews.jp/article/f020010900011000000000000000W0591010111000020344A Walking all the "walkable bridges" of the Sumida River] Asahi Shimbun Withnews</ref>.
== History == === Edo Period === * 1594 (Bunroku 3): {{ill|Ina Tadatsugu|en|Ina Tadatsugu|WD=Q3387913|lt=Ina Tadatsugu}}, the Kantō Governor, constructs the [[:ja:千住大橋|Senju Bridge]]. * 1661 (Kanbun 1): In response to the {{ill|Great Fire of Meireki|en|Great Fire of Meireki|WD=Q1336847|lt=Great Fire of Meireki}}, the Tokugawa Shogunate constructs the {{ill|Ryōgoku_Bridge|en|Ryōgoku_Bridge|WD=Q3080489|lt=Ryōgoku Bridge}}. * During the Genroku Era: The shogunate constructs the [[:ja:新大橋|Shin-Ōhashi]] and [[:ja:永代橋|Eitai Bridge]]. * 1774 (An'ei 3): The town council builds the [[:ja:吾妻橋|Azuma Bridge]]. <gallery> File:100 views edo 103.jpg|Senju Bridge File:Brooklyn Museum - Ryogoku Bridge and the Great Riverbank No 59 from One Hundred Views of Edo - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|Ryōgoku Bridge File:Hiroshige Van Gogh 2.JPG|Shin-Ōhashi File:100 views edo 004.jpg|Eitai Bridge File:100 views edo 055.jpg|Azuma Bridge </gallery>
=== Meiji Period === * 1872 (Meiji 5): The Meiji Government designates Eitai Bridge, Shin-Ōhashi, Ryōgoku Bridge, and Azuma Bridge as state-managed bridges. * 1874 (Meiji 7): The [[:ja:厩橋|Kuramae Bridge]] is constructed. * 1903 (Meiji 36): The [[:ja:相生橋_(東京都)|Aioi Bridge]] is constructed.
=== Taishō Period === [[File:Kiyosubashi bridge.jpg|thumb|The reconstructed Kiyosu Bridge, praised as the most modern and beautiful among the Sumida Ten Bridges]] * 1913 (Taishō 3): Construction of the [[Shirahige Bridge]]. * After the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|en|Great Kantō Earthquake|WD=Q274498|lt=Great Kantō Earthquake}}, wooden bridges are destroyed by fire, trapping evacuees and leading to a disaster. Plans are made to construct iron bridges: ** The [[:ja:内務省復興局|Home Ministry Reconstruction Bureau]] reconstructs six bridges (Eitai Bridge, [[:ja:清洲橋|Kiyosu Bridge]], [[:ja:蔵前橋|Kuramae Bridge]], [[:ja:駒形橋|Komagata Bridge]], [[:ja:言問橋|Kototoi Bridge]], and Aioi Bridge). ** The {{ill|Tokyo_City|en|Tokyo_City|WD=Q1207735|lt=Tokyo City Government}} reconstructs Ryōgoku Bridge, Kuramae Bridge, and Azuma Bridge.
=== Early Shōwa Period === * During the {{ill|Bombing_of_Tokyo|en|Bombing_of_Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo Air Raids}}, the bridges served as critical evacuation routes.
=== Postwar to Heisei Period === * The Sumida River bridges continued to play a vital role in Tokyo’s urban development and infrastructure.
== Current Major Road Bridges == === Prewar Bridges === ; Important Cultural Properties {{col| * Eitai Bridge * Kiyosu Bridge | * [[Kachidoki Bridge]] }} ; Tokyo Designated Historical Buildings {{col| * Kuramae Bridge * Kuramae Bridge * Komagata Bridge * Azuma Bridge | * [[Shirahige Bridge]] * Ryōgoku Bridge * Kototoi Bridge }} ; Other Bridges * [[Senju Bridge]] (Old Bridge)
=== Postwar Bridges === {{col| * Senju Bridge (New Bridge) * [[Suijin Bridge]] * [[Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|Sakura Bridge]] * [[Shin-Ōhashi]] | * [[Sumida River Bridge]] * Aioi Bridge * [[Chūō Bridge]] * [[Tsukuda Bridge]] }}
